item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"The SCP Foundation"

In-universe, it's just "The Foundation" (Though, there's no canon so go nuts.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The core concept is interesting, and the comparison between Foundationverse practices with humanity more generally is cool. However the article does not fill it out well enough. The audio log by Irantu doesn't feel very in-character (I appreciate that they're ideatically displaced, my concern is with the verisimilitude). Just describing the different levels and having somebody narrate it doesn't make me feel the true scope of what this anomaly is and why it's important.

Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very confusing but still equally entertaining. Nice touch with the Enochian OC, too. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Generally meta/pata/whatever-physical themes tend to go over my head but I feel this one was explained well enough, and with simple enough terms, that I didn't have much trouble understanding the main idea. The relatively short length of it helps with this since I never felt like I was lost as the narrative progressed.

The one part I'm not getting completely is this bit from the final note:

Per general Overwatch consensus (See: Minutes.4755.2005-37), failure to complete Operation Almeresh has been deemed optimal, so as to instigate a containment failure of the SCP Foundation.

I know that it has to do with the ethical concerns mentioned during the logs, but I'm not quite sure why this is the most optimal course of action.

One minor nitpick is the Lucifer part. While it's true the passage where the word lucifer is mentioned might be alluding to Satan's fall, it's not supposed to be a name. Aside from King James Bible, and derivatives, lucifer is either a descriptor or just an object. Jesus is even called lucifer as in "light bringer" in an older hymm/psalm. Irrelevant to the rest of the article, but it did stand out to me so I might as well mention it.

Either way, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad, this is a scp that holds well without referring to another prior material in this case, your Site-17 hub.

I would upvote it, but there are two reasons for me:

1. I prefer 4096 more. I like it when the Foundation doesn't have much control.
2. Personal opinion, but lately I don't like much settings where the Foundation sucks or is a crapsack world again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's so weird, but so fun. It kind of pulls off that thing where you don't understand all the terms or science, but you can still follow along with what's happening. It really is an impressive feat to pull off.

+1, easily.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

This broke my head when I first read it and filled me with dread and longing. In a good way. This is the Foundation Obsession with Containment taken to the illogical extreme; out into conceptual space, in the world of Forms. I cannot but admire it.

I do worry this will break some people, that its grandiosity will be seen as controversial or large for largeness sake. But the story is relayed in a comprehensible and emotional way, such that I feel the growing horror of the modern Foundation in the face of what they become. It's astonishing, and very well done.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
I'm getting tired of hearing about how the Foundation's efforts are futile. That you can't put the world in a box, that the Veil is unsustainable, that it's a boom-and-bust cycle of containment epitomized by a snake biting its own tail.

Do you honestly believe the Foundation couldn't pull it off?

We godfellas now. ☄️☀️🌋


A special thank-you goes to Francis Scalia, Calibri Bold, and FortuneFavorsBold for their help and inspiration. Omega Fallon created the Enochian icon, I made that glowing image by the way, so I suppose it's compliant.

Stay tuned for more on this end. If you're looking for info on Enochian, it's been used in a few joke articles and one SCP on the French branch. To paraphrase SCP-340-FR, "Item cannot be contained due to its properties constituting baseline reality."

P.S.: Can't be bothered to get the Site-17 logo to work on Black Highlighter, so SCP-4755 says trans rights now. 🏳️‍🌈
